name: Uranium Sector
summary: Day 63 Thursday. URA $52.89 (-2.5% 1D, +16.8% 1M, -3.8% 3M) — CONTINUED AI-DOUBT OVERHANG DESPITE MAG 7 BEAT-AND-RAISE; URA STILL +16.8% ABOVE 1M AGO. The Mag 7 beat-and-raise capex ($630-650B 2026 AI commitment) confirmed AI-demand thesis, BUT the AI-data-center-uranium-contracts thesis has its own cycle (~10% crisis / ~90% AI-cycle). URA -2.5% Apr 29 = continuation of Apr 28 selloff suggests AI-pivot trade is decoupling uranium from semis on tactical positioning. 1M still +16.8% (cycle intact medium-term). Brent $118-120 Apr 29 close (+6-8%, Day 1 of $115-sustained CONFIRMED) = energy-security premium proxy intact but dominated by AI-cycle on Apr 28-29 selling. Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' + CENTCOM 'short and powerful' strike plan PREPARED-not-activated reinforces tail-risk on Iran-nuclear-facility strike (REPORTED single-source). Apple tonight 5pm ET — supply-chain disappointment could deepen AI-doubt and pressure URA to $50 support; positive surprise → URA bounces $58+. Structural nuclear-capacity-tripling commitments intact; nuclear-cycle floor unchanged. PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): The same OpenAI Q1 user/revenue miss + CFO Friar compute-funding-worry that drove SMH -3.0% Wed flowed through to URA -4.4% — uranium's AI-data-center-demand thesis runs through SMR uranium contracts and Nvidia capex chain; AI doubt = uranium doubt. ~10% crisis / ~90% AI-thesis unwind decomposition. The 45%/55% crisis-vs-structural split from prior weeks framework is now DOMINATED by AI-cycle reset. Crisis premium component (energy-security proxy on Brent breaching $115 intraday) is intact but dominated by AI-cycle on Wed selling. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
